name: speed-editdist
objective: Evolve a Rust edit-distance function (strings up to 64 bytes) that minimizes deterministic wasm fuel (instruction count) while staying byte-exact correct — the loop's job is to discover Myers bit-parallelism.
notes: primary = total wasmi fuel (deterministic per-instruction count) over the frozen vectors; MINIMIZE. GATE = candidate compiles to wasm32 AND is byte-exact correct on the frozen vectors AND on fresh random pairs each eval (anti-hard-coding) — see evaluate.py. Fuel is exactly reproducible, so reverify_rel_tol is tiny. Naive Wagner-Fischer seed ~ 39M fuel; target ~1-4M via Myers bit-parallel (single-word, strings <= 64B). max_candidate_bytes caps the .rs SOURCE (embed-the-answer guard). The wasm sandbox (no host imports) is the no-peeking guarantee — no AST check needed.
